author: cursor-agent

patch.value: Track universal terminal compatibility improvements and rollout evidence.
Harden pm CLI stdin, exit, and subprocess behavior for reliability across IDE-integrated and emulated terminals (VS Code forks, ConPTY, PTY shells) without regressions.
pm CLI behaves deterministically across terminal types, avoids stdin hang footguns, preserves exit-code/output contracts, and ships with comprehensive regression tests and docs.
Research and duplicate scan are complete with implementation scope mapped.
Make pm robust across emulated terminals
Fewer hangs and misdetected outcomes for agents and users
Higher command reliability across VS Code-like environments
Stable stdin/output/exit behavior with no regressions
Terminal emulation differences currently cause hangs and unreliable command interpretation.
Improves reliability for all users running pm in IDE or emulated terminals.

message: Reopen epic for PTY reliability follow-up after reproduced silent long-run test-all behavior.

author: cursor-agent

value.author: cursor-agent
value.text: Follow-up continuation of this existing terminal epic (no duplicate item created). Reproduced residual behavior on 2026-04-01: pm test-all --status in_progress --timeout 60 --json remained silent for about 170s before final output/exit while linked tests were active. Acceptance delta for reopen: spawn-based linked-test orchestration, default stderr heartbeat/progress, and deterministic timeout tree termination across PTY environments.

message: Record follow-up regression evidence and acceptance delta for reopened terminal epic.

author: cursor-agent
message: Claim terminal hardening epic for closure.

author: cursor-agent

value.text: Reproduced and validated PTY reliability follow-up end to end: script -q -c "pm test-all --status in_progress --timeout 60 --json" /tmp/pm-test-all-pty.log now shows continuous [pm test] linked-test heartbeat progress lines and deterministic completion payload instead of long silent intervals. Full regression remains green (node scripts/run-tests.mjs test: 693/693).
